text: 1) Physics – Short Answer Planck was able to describe the color change of black bodies at different temperatures by introducing what concept?
label: TOSSUP_TEXT

text: ANSWER: QUANTUM (ACCEPT: QUANTA, QUANTIZATION; DO NOT ACCEPT: PHOTON)
label: TOSSUP_ANSWER

text: 1) Physics – Short Answer A horizontal force of 90 newtons is applied to a 20-kilogram block that is initially at rest on a horizontal surface. The coefficient of static friction between the block and the surface is 0.5 and the coefficient of kinetic friction of 0.2. In meters per second squared, what is the acceleration of the block?
label: BONUS_TEXT

text: 4) Biology – Multiple Choice What is the probability of obtaining an F1 purple pea flower if purple is dominant, white is recessive, and a parental cross is made between a heterozygous purple and homozygous white flower? W) 1/8 X) 1/4 Y) 1/2 Z) 1

text: 5) Math – Multiple Choice Matrices with which of the following pairs of dimensions cannot be multiplied together? W) 3 rows, 2 columns times 2 rows, 3 columns X) 6 rows, 5 columns times 6 rows, 5 columns Y) 1 row, 2 columns times 2 rows, 2 columns Z) 1,000 rows, 2,000 columns times 2,000 rows, 4,000 columns
label: TOSSUP_TEXT

text: ANSWER: X) 6 ROWS, 5 COLUMNS TIMES 6 ROWS, 5 COLUMNS
label: TOSSUP_ANSWER

text: 5) Math – Short Answer Evaluate the following expression: (−cos 360° + sin 270°) × (cos 0° + sin 0°)^2

text: 7) Energy – Short Answer Uranium pellets that are sealed into fuel rods are composed of what specific uranium compound?
label: TOSSUP_TEXT

text: ANSWER: URANIUM DIOXIDE (ACCEPT: URANIUM(IV) OXIDE, URANOUS OXIDE, URANIA; DO NOT ACCEPT: URANIUM OXIDE)
label: TOSSUP_ANSWER

text: 7) Energy – Multiple Choice What technology provides the vast majority of the worldwide grid energy storage capacity? W) Liquid or compressed air X) Lead-acid, nickel-cadmium and sodium-sulfur batteries Y) Hydroelectric pump storage Z) Superconducting magnetic energy storage
label: BONUS_TEXT

text: 8) Biology – Multiple Choice Which of the following best describes how oxygen is stored in the muscles? W) Spread throughout X) Bound to hemoglobin Y) Bound to calmodulin Z) Bound to myoglobin
label: TOSSUP_TEXT

text: 8) Biology – Multiple Choice Which of the following examples is NOT evidence for evolution from a common ancestor? W) All living things have basic cell processes that are similar and use similar molecules X) Hox genes, or homeotic genes, control vertebrate body plans and are homologous in all vertebrates Y) Even though snakes don't have legs, some snake embryos have limb buds during development similar to other reptile embryos Z) Bats and birds both have wings that are adaptations for flying
label: BONUS_TEXT

text: ANSWER: Z) BATS AND BIRDS BOTH HAVE WINGS THAT ARE ADAPTATIONS FOR FLYING
label: BONUS_ANSWER

text: 2016 NSB® Regional High School Questions    Page 6
label: META

text: 9) Physics – Multiple Choice Which of the following is NOT true of laser light? W) It is highly coherent X) It is highly dispersive Y) It is highly monochromatic Z) It is highly directional
label: TOSSUP_TEXT

text: ANSWER: X) IT IS HIGHLY DISPERSIVE
label: TOSSUP_ANSWER

text: 11) Math – Short Answer There are 8 siblings in a family, and when ranked from youngest to oldest, all differ from the next oldest in age by the same number of years. If the youngest is 8 and the oldest is 29, what is the common difference in their ages?
label: TOSSUP_TEXT

text: 11) Math – Multiple Choice Following are four pairs of intersecting lines that are related to circles. For three of them, the measures of the angles formed can always be found by taking half of the difference of the angle measures of the intercepted arcs. For which does this calculation generally fail? W) Two tangents X) Two secants that intersect outside the circle Y) A tangent and a secant that intersect outside the circle Z) The lines containing two intersecting chords
label: BONUS_TEXT

text: ANSWER: Z) THE LINES CONTAINING TWO INTERSECTING CHORDS
label: BONUS_ANSWER

text: 15) Physics – Short Answer A helicopter uses a main rotor as propulsion and control, but requires a tail rotor to counteract torque. The balance of forces resulting from this configuration is represented best by which of Newton's laws?
label: TOSSUP_TEXT

text: ANSWER: THIRD (ACCEPT: FOR EVERY ACTION, THERE IS AN EQUAL AND OPPOSITE REACTION)
label: TOSSUP_ANSWER

text: 17) Chemistry – Short Answer What principle of chemical equilibrium states that if a chemical system at equilibrium experiences a change in concentration, temperature, or pressure, the equilibrium will shift in order to counteract that change?
label: TOSSUP_TEXT

text: 17) Chemistry – Multiple Choice Consider both the hydrogenation and the bromination of alkenes using elemental bromine. Which of the following statements concerning these reactions is TRUE? W) Both reactions are syn-additions X) Hydrogenation is a syn-addition, but bromination is an anti-addition Y) Hydrogenation is an anti-addition, but bromination is a syn-addition Z) Both reactions are anti-additions
label: BONUS_TEXT

text: ANSWER: X) HYDROGENATION IS A SYN-ADDITION, BUT BROMINATION IS AN ANTI ADDITION
label: BONUS_ANSWER

text: 22) Biology – Multiple Choice Which of the following is NOT true about plant physiology? W) Meristematic tissue located at the tips of stems and roots forms the apical meristems X) Some plants, like shrubs, have lateral meristems Y) Vascular and cork cambium are produced by apical meristems Z) Intercalary meristems allow grass to grow even after it has been mowed
label: TOSSUP_TEXT

text: ANSWER: Y) VASCULAR AND CORK CAMBIUM ARE PRODUCED BY APICAL MERISTEMS
label: TOSSUP_ANSWER
